title: Best VPN for Germany 2026: Top Picks for Privacy, Streaming and Torrenting
description: The best VPN for Germany in 2026: NordVPN for speed, ExpressVPN for streaming German Netflix, Mullvad for maximum privacy. Full comparison with Germany-specific details.
content:
The best VPN for Germany in 2026 is NordVPN for most users, ExpressVPN for accessing German-exclusive streaming content from abroad, and Mullvad for users who want the strongest privacy protections. Germany has one of Europe's strictest digital environments: robust copyright enforcement, ISP data retention laws, and aggressive anti-piracy legal notices (Abmahnungen) make a VPN genuinely useful here, not just a nice-to-have.

Why You Need a VPN in Germany

Germany's internet landscape has specific risks that make VPN protection more valuable than in most Western European countries.

Copyright enforcement and Abmahnungen: Germany has some of the strictest copyright enforcement in Europe. Law firms routinely send legal demand letters (Abmahnungen) to IP addresses that appear in torrenting swarms. Fines of 500 to 2,000 EUR per infringement are common. A VPN masks your IP from copyright monitoring agencies, which only see the VPN server's address, not yours.

Data retention laws: Germany has repeatedly attempted to implement mandatory ISP data retention (Vorratsdatenspeicherung) under EU pressure. While courts have struck down blanket retention multiple times, the legal situation remains contested. A VPN encrypts traffic before it reaches your ISP, preventing connection-level logging regardless of how the law evolves.

Accessing German content from abroad: ARD Mediathek, ZDF Mediathek, RTL+, and Joyn require a German IP address. German Netflix has a separate catalog. If you live or travel outside Germany, these services are geo-blocked without a Germany-based VPN server.

Public Wi-Fi security: Germany has dense public Wi-Fi coverage in train stations, cafes, and city centers. These networks are unencrypted. A VPN protects your banking, email, and personal data on these hotspots.

Best VPN Providers for Germany (2026)

1. NordVPN (Best Overall)

NordVPN has over 200 servers in Germany, located in Frankfurt and Berlin. Frankfurt is one of the largest internet exchange points in the world (DE-CIX), which means NordVPN's German servers have extremely low latency connections to the global internet backbone. Speed tests on German fiber connections (FTTH) regularly show 200 to 400 Mbps using the NordLynx (WireGuard-based) protocol, which adds minimal overhead on fast connections.

NordVPN holds independent no-logs audits from PricewaterhouseCoopers (2023) and Deloitte (2024). Its threat protection feature blocks tracking scripts, malware domains, and ads at the DNS level before they reach your device. It works with Netflix Germany, ARD Mediathek, and ZDF Mediathek reliably. Price: from 3.09 USD per month on a two-year plan. Allows ten simultaneous connections. Best choice for most German users and for accessing German content from abroad.

2. ExpressVPN (Best for German Streaming)

ExpressVPN has Germany servers in Frankfurt and consistently unblocks ARD Mediathek, ZDF Mediathek, RTL+, and Netflix Germany from outside the country. Its MediaStreamer Smart DNS feature allows streaming on devices that cannot run a VPN natively, including Smart TVs, Apple TV, and older game consoles.

Speed averages 150 to 250 Mbps on German servers with a standard broadband connection. More expensive at 6.67 USD per month on an annual plan. The extra cost is justified if streaming German content from abroad is your main use case, as ExpressVPN has the most consistent record of staying ahead of streaming platform VPN detection.

3. Mullvad (Best for Privacy)

Mullvad was founded in Gothenburg, Sweden, by privacy advocates and has an unusually strong stance on user data. You sign up with a randomly generated account number and no email address. Payment options include cash sent by post and Monero cryptocurrency. In 2023, Swedish police raided Mullvad's offices and left empty-handed because the company had no logs to hand over, confirming their no-logs policy is genuine.

Mullvad has servers in Frankfurt and uses WireGuard with DAITA (Defence Against AI-guided Traffic Analysis), which adds randomized traffic patterns to prevent behavioral fingerprinting. Price: 5 EUR per month flat, no annual discount, no free trial. Best suited for journalists, activists, lawyers, or anyone who needs to confirm their VPN provider holds no data about them.

4. Surfshark (Best Budget Option)

Surfshark offers unlimited simultaneous connections and Germany servers (Frankfurt) for 2.19 USD per month on a two-year plan. Good for households with many devices. Speed averages 120 to 180 Mbps on German servers. Surfshark also includes CleanWeb (ad and tracker blocking) and Nexus (routing traffic through multiple countries) in the base subscription. The lack of a per-device limit makes it uniquely cost-effective for families.

5. Proton VPN (Best Free + Privacy Combination)

Proton VPN is based in Switzerland (outside EU data sharing agreements) and has servers in Frankfurt. The free tier has no data cap and no speed throttling on free servers, though free server selection is limited. The paid plan (Proton VPN Plus, from 4 EUR per month) adds Stealth protocol (which disguises VPN traffic as regular HTTPS), full-speed German servers, and Tor-over-VPN.

Proton has a Warrant Canary and has published transparency reports since 2018. Its no-logs policy has been audited by Securitum. For users who want Swiss-jurisdiction privacy protections and a credible free tier, Proton VPN is the strongest option.

How to Access German Streaming Services from Abroad

The major German public broadcasters (ARD, ZDF, 3sat) and private channels (RTL, ProSieben, Sat.1) are geo-blocked outside Germany. Here is how to access each:

ARD Mediathek

ARD Mediathek blocks non-German IP addresses. Connect to a VPN Germany server before loading the site or app. NordVPN, ExpressVPN, and Surfshark all work reliably with ARD Mediathek as of July 2026. The ARD app on mobile does not always respect a VPN; using a browser on the mediathek.ard.de website is more reliable.

ZDF Mediathek

ZDF Mediathek uses the same IP-based geo-blocking as ARD. Connect to a Germany server first. ZDF also operates funk.net, a youth content platform with the same geo-restriction. Both work with the VPNs listed above.

RTL+ and ProSieben

RTL+ (formerly TV Now) requires a German IP and a paid RTL+ subscription. ProSieben's Joyn streaming service similarly requires a German IP. Both unblock with NordVPN or ExpressVPN Germany servers.

Netflix Germany

Netflix Germany has a different content library from Netflix US or UK, including German-language originals (Dark, Barbarians, How to Sell Drugs Online Fast) and licensed content not available in other regions. Connect to a Germany server, then open Netflix. If you get a proxy error (Error M7111-5059), switch to a different Germany server. NordVPN has the most Germany servers to rotate through.

VPN and Torrenting in Germany

Torrenting copyrighted material in Germany without a VPN carries a real financial risk. Anti-piracy organizations such as GEMA and private law firms including Waldorf Frommer monitor torrenting swarms and collect IP addresses. They match those IPs to ISP customer records via court orders and send Abmahnungen demanding payment.

A VPN with P2P-optimized servers routes your torrent traffic through the VPN server's IP address. Monitoring agencies see the VPN IP, not yours. To minimize risk when torrenting in Germany:

  • Use NordVPN's P2P-optimized servers (clearly labeled in the app). These are located in countries without aggressive copyright enforcement.
  • Enable the kill switch. If the VPN connection drops, the kill switch cuts all internet access to prevent your real IP from appearing in the torrent swarm.
  • Use a torrent client that binds to the VPN network interface, not your default connection. qBittorrent supports interface binding under Settings > Advanced > Network interface.
  • Avoid German VPN servers for torrenting. Even if your IP is masked, routing through Germany adds legal jurisdiction risk.

Is a VPN Legal in Germany?

Yes, VPNs are fully legal in Germany for personal and business use. The German Federal Ministry of Justice has not restricted VPN use. Companies commonly use VPNs for remote work, and personal use for privacy or streaming is not illegal.

Using a VPN to access geo-restricted streaming content may violate a streaming service's terms of service, but this is a contractual matter between you and the service, not a criminal or civil legal issue under German law. The service can cancel your account, but you face no legal liability.

Data Retention Laws in Germany: What You Need to Know

Germany's history with data retention (Vorratsdatenspeicherung) is complex. The European Court of Justice struck down blanket mandatory data retention in 2022 as incompatible with EU law. Germany subsequently passed a revised

law (Sicherheitsgesetz II) in 2024, requiring targeted retention on specific accounts when law enforcement requests it, rather than blanket logging of all users.

A VPN operates regardless of these legal frameworks. Your ISP sees only encrypted VPN traffic with no content or destination metadata. Even under a targeted freeze, your ISP's logs would show only that you connected to a VPN server, not what you did on the other side of it. The VPN provider's no-logs policy determines what the VPN company itself could hand over if legally compelled. For Mullvad and Proton VPN, independent audits confirm those logs do not exist.

Free vs Paid VPN for Germany

Free VPNs are inadequate for German-specific needs for several reasons:

  • No P2P support: Free VPN tiers almost never allow torrenting. If copyright protection is your primary reason for using a VPN in Germany, a free VPN does not solve the problem.
  • Data caps: Most free VPNs cap data at 500 MB to 2 GB per month. Streaming ARD Mediathek uses roughly 1 GB per hour.
  • No Germany servers: Many free VPNs have only US and a few EU servers. Germany-specific servers require infrastructure investment that free tiers do not fund.
  • Privacy concerns: Several popular free VPN apps have been caught selling user data or logging connections. This defeats the entire purpose in Germany's legal environment.

Proton VPN Free is the only legitimate free option with decent speeds and a verified no-logs policy. It has German servers on the free tier. For anything beyond basic privacy browsing, a paid plan at 2 to 5 EUR per month is a better investment given what is at stake legally in Germany.

How to Set Up a VPN in Germany

Setup takes under five minutes on any device:

  1. Choose a provider. For most users: NordVPN. For streaming priority: ExpressVPN. For maximum privacy: Mullvad.
  2. Download the app from the provider's official website (not a third-party app store to avoid spoofed apps).
  3. Create an account and subscribe.
  4. Open the app and connect to a Germany server for German content access, or a non-Germany server for torrent privacy.
  5. Verify your IP changed by visiting whatismyip.com or ipleak.net after connecting.
  6. Enable the kill switch in the app settings. This is critical in Germany if you use the VPN for copyright-sensitive activity.

Frequently Asked Questions

What is the best VPN for Germany in 2026?

NordVPN is the best overall VPN for Germany in 2026. It has 200-plus German servers in Frankfurt and Berlin, a verified no-logs policy, and fast NordLynx speeds. For streaming German content from abroad, ExpressVPN is the most reliable choice.

Can I use a VPN to watch ARD Mediathek outside Germany?

Yes. Connect to a Germany server before opening ARD Mediathek. NordVPN, ExpressVPN, and Surfshark all unblock ARD Mediathek as of July 2026. Use the website (mediathek.ard.de) rather than the app for the most consistent results.

Is torrenting with a VPN safe in Germany?

A VPN with a kill switch significantly reduces the risk of an Abmahnung by hiding your real IP from copyright monitoring agencies. Use P2P-optimized servers outside Germany and enable the kill switch. NordVPN and Mullvad are the best options for this use case.

Is a VPN legal in Germany?

Yes, VPNs are completely legal in Germany for personal and business use. There are no restrictions on VPN software or protocols. Using a VPN for privacy, streaming, or remote work is lawful.

What is an Abmahnung and how does a VPN protect against it?

An Abmahnung is a cease-and-desist letter with a monetary demand sent by law firms representing copyright holders. They obtain your IP address from torrent monitoring services, then subpoena your ISP for your name and address. A VPN hides your real IP so monitoring agencies record the VPN server's IP, which the VPN provider cannot link to your identity if they hold no logs.

Does NordVPN have servers in Germany?

Yes. NordVPN has over 200 servers in Germany in Frankfurt and Berlin. These are among NordVPN's highest-traffic servers due to Germany's position on the DE-CIX internet exchange. Available on all NordVPN plans.

Which VPN is best for accessing ZDF Mediathek from abroad?

NordVPN and ExpressVPN both reliably unblock ZDF Mediathek from outside Germany as of July 2026. Connect to a Germany server before opening the site. If one server gives an error, switch to another Germany server in the same app.
